senses.gloss: dragging along behind oneself, marching heavily
tug, a vehicle that pulls things such as a tractor or tugboat
deathstalker (Compsobuthus pallidus and Leiurus (?), a scorpion which drags its prey on its tail behind itself)

examples.text: الجرّارات وهي عقارب صغار تجرّ أذنابها وتكون ببلاد الخوز ويقال لها بالنبطيّة كرورا
examples.english: jarrārāt are little scorpions dragging along their tails found in Khuzistan and called in Nabataean gərōrā.

etymology.text:
Characteristic adjective from جَرَّ (jarra, “to pull, to drag”).

Occupational noun of جَرَّة (jarra, “jar”).
